message:
Fix method-call-in-expression bug for custom classes

Fixes bug where method calls in expression context (print, assignment,
return) would disappear from generated code, leaving empty expressions.

Root cause (identified by Agent 1):
- dispatch.zig routes ALL .get() calls to methods.genGet() (dict handler)
- methods.genGet() expects dict.get(key) with >=1 argument
- When called with 0 args (counter.get()), it returned early without output
- Result: print(counter.get()) generated print({}) with missing call

Fix (implemented by Agent 2):
- When genGet() sees 0 args, generate generic method call: obj.get()
- This handles custom class methods that happen to be named 'get'
- Preserves dict.get(key) behavior for >=1 args

Impact (tested by Agent 3):
- 14/89 → 22/89 tests passing (+57% improvement!)
- 8 new passing tests:
  * class_simple, class_multiple (class functionality)
  * function_basic, function_factorial, function_multiple (functions)
  * builtin_len_min_max_sum, mymath, strutils (other improvements)

Working examples:
✅ Assignment: x = counter.get()
✅ Print: print(counter.get())
✅ Nested: self.get() + self.get()
✅ Return: return counter.get()

Before fix:
```python
print(counter.get())
```
Generated: `std.debug.print("{any}\n", .{});`  ← Empty!

After fix:
Generated: `std.debug.print("{any}\n", .{counter.get()});`  ✅

Known limitations:
- Still affects other method names (append, split, etc.) - will need
  type-aware dispatch for full fix
- Type inference for classes without annotations (anytype error)
- String printing shows bytes instead of formatted text

Files modified:
- src/codegen/native/methods.zig (+8 lines)

This was a CRITICAL bug blocking class adoption. With this fix, basic
classes now work in all expression contexts.

Implemented by: 3 parallel agents (Agent 1: debug, Agent 2: fix,
Agent 3: test)
